---
title: "Applications"
---

Where does circular structure already live in the real world? Schedules repeat. Signals oscillate. Buffers wrap. Positions in a transformer rotate. The applications unit is a tour of domains where periodic, rotational, or phase-like structure is *already present* — and where making it explicit lets you test and verify it.

::: {.lesson-goal}

## Unit Goal

For each domain, identify its circular address space and its repeated action, and learn to separate the Lean-proved finite seeds from the claims that still need benchmarks.

:::

## The Idea

Every application reuses the same small vocabulary from S1:

```text
address -> stride -> orbit -> period -> phase -> checked status
```

The claim is *not* that circular language automatically improves every domain. It is narrower and more honest: where the structure is genuinely periodic, an explicit finite representation can be checked, and a handful of finite facts can be Lean-proved. Benchmarks and fixtures are executable references, not proof of real-world performance or usefulness — so for each page, ask:

1. What is the circular address space?
2. What is the stride, phase, schedule, or repeated action?
3. Which claims are Lean-proved finite seeds, and which still require benchmarks?

## Case-Study Tracks

- [Classical bridges](erdos_bridges.qmd) — Erdős-style combinatorics restated through Circle vocabulary (Lean-checked wrappers, not new results).
- [AI](ai.qmd) — phase channels, cyclic memory, coil retrieval, routing, and adapter-block indexing seeds.
- [AI contract ladder](ai_contract_ladder.qmd) — guided sequence from RoPE phase certificates to KV-cache freshness, sparse-attention coverage, and circulant mixer guarantees.
- [RoPE certifier](rope_certifier.qmd) — proof-carrying position-distinguishability certificates for exact discretized rotary phase banks.
- [KV-cache ring buffer](kv_cache_ring_buffer.qmd) — proof-carrying finite slot and retained-window certificates for ring-buffer cache indexing.
- [Sparse attention coverage](sparse_attention_contract.qmd) — proof-carrying finite coverage, gap-witness, and candidate-budget certificates for local-window plus stride-family sparse plans.
- [Physics](physics.qmd) — finite gauge links, path holonomy, Wilson-loop certificates, plaquettes.
- [Generative structures](generative.qmd) — seed-rule provenance and exact-regeneration fixtures.
- [Compute](compute.qmd) — cyclic addresses, direction bins, stride layouts, round-robin schedules.
- [Rendering](rendering.qmd) — direction-bin sampling vocabulary.
- [Data analysis](data_analysis.qmd) — finite phase coordinates and synthetic periodic-signal fixtures.

::: {.warning-box}
These are active but exploratory tracks: theorem cards are the status source, and benchmarks and fixtures are executable references, not proof of real-world performance or usefulness. See [What "Proved" Means Here](../../what_proved_means.qmd).
:::

::: {.checkpoint-box}

## Checkpoint

Pick one application page and name its finite circular address space before reading any theorem card.

:::

## Representative Proved Seeds

<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="APPD-T0005"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="COMPC-T0005"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="COMPR-T0005"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="COMPL-T0005"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="COMPS-T0004"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="AIA-T0005"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="AIM-T0005"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="AIM-T0010"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="AIM-T0069"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="AIM-T0073"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="AIRA-T0005"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="AIRA-T0024"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="AIT-T0078"></div>

<div class="book-navigation">
<a href="../phase2/proof_carrying_glyphs.qmd">Previous: Proof-Carrying Glyphs</a>
<a href="erdos_bridges.qmd">Next: Classical Bridges</a>
</div>

<script type="module" src="../../widgets/shared/widget_base.js"></script>
